title_guess: Italy/Switzerland 1993: via Ogbourne St George, Portsmouth, St Gotthard, Lugano, Cremona, Parma

pages.text:
April 16th 1993 Friday.

We set off from home at 2.40. on Charles' birthday.
    Simon 43, [J]Eve 39, Thomas 13, William 11, Peter 9, John 5, Charles 4.
    It is the last weekend of the Easter holidays.
    We stopped in a lay-by for Charle[s'] chocolate birthday cake.
    The Ogbourne St. George ****
was very pretty, big open chalk fields, hills, at a distance, all roun[d]
Only one chemical toilet, John got out & ran up the hill. Thomas & Pete[r] slept in the tent. John started off in the [to]p bunk & then moved to the bottom. [I]ve got a metal splinter from the torch in the middle of the night. Maglite

April 17th.
	We visited Di + Ian. She opened the door with "Hello! Would you like a drink + a pee?" We had elevenses + drove on to

[sketch of David + Maxine's House, Newbury, labelled: kitchen, eating room]

David + Maxine's House, Newbury.
[?Bley Lodge]

Hungerford. We wandered around antique shops, Simon looking for dining room chairs (unsuccessful) + Eve looking for hair pins (successful), and on for lunch + the night to David + Maxines + Imogen 3½ + Iona 2 at Newbury.
	We went on a long walk past "The & 5 Bells, very pretty, to the Watermill Theatre – lovely auditorium, + back for tea.
	Bgoats in the field, kite flying.
	Thomas + William slept at the bottom of the garden.
	Imogen, Iona, John + Charles rolled down the hill in the caterpillar + the little [illegible].

April 18th
	We had breakfast – gorgeous fry-up in the house, + left for Hattie + James at Chandlers Ford. We had a delicious roast lunch, + chatted. James caught a bus to Heathrow, plane to Rome + then Cairo, then minibus!

Alexandra, working for Cunard personel.
Anna came back from her job as a chamber-
maid. Katrina was doing community service at an
old peoples' home.
	Simon drove all of us + Hattie +
Katrina, Laura + Edward to see __Winchester__
__Cathedral__. It was lovely to see all the
Dugoids. Simon + Eve slept in the new extension.

	__April 19th__
	Hattie left for work on her first day
of term, having calmly given everyone breakfast.
Then we set off for __Romsey Rapids__.
	The Rapids £11-20 for 1 adult,
5 children, were great fun. We had
hot chocolate afterwards.
	We drove to __Portsmouth__ + ate in the
carpark, surrounded by hoardes of French
students.
	The HMS Victory, Nelson's flagship
was fascinating. The guide pretended Peter
[?to] [illegible] at 10 miles per hour

captions: Bell tower Portsmouth carpark.  St Anne's Chu[rch]
pages.text:
Bell tower Portsmouth carpark.  St Anne's Chu[rch]

[Hand-drawn sketch of a bell tower / cupola with weather vane (showing S and N compass points) above a domed roof supported by columns, sitting atop a square tower base, with a flat rooftop in the foreground]

was the "powder monkey", sitting on a biscuit barrel, responsible for the 14 men in his group. 8 pints beer once they were 14. 500 of the 800 men slept on one deck. 60% press ganged. No room to swing a cat.
	Then we looked at the Mary Rose being sprayed, a wax which will impregnate the wood. She should have had only 400 men, but 700 were on her the day she sank. Sir George Carew couldn't control them.
	Ropes went out the stern of the ship from a reel, showing how many "knots" the speed went.
	Shoes, clothes, medical equipment, cooking, arrows, bows, guns.

captions: Angels carved in bone, found in a chest on the main deck, may have formed part of the spine of a book.  Mary Rose
pages.text: Angels carved in bone, found in a chest on the main deck, may have formed part of the spine of a book.		Mary Rose

We drove on into Surrey & camped near Leatherhead. Horsley *** the site was very wet, boggy grass & a lake, but had a shop & was friendly. Supper, lovely in Indian restaurant.
__Tues Apl 20th__
   We set off into a Danish shop in Leatherhead & Simon rescued his lights. Meanwhile Thomas & Eve were doing battle with a fractious John & Charles & exceedingly irate shop-owner to get sandwiches for lunch.
   We drove on to London & Thomas found money for the metre for the van parked in __Finsbury Circus__, while Simon tried to mend his lamps on a building being renovated. William got everyone into Finsbury Circus Park for a picnic. (The oldest park in the city - the "fen where water went under the Roman wall - made into a park in 1600s.)
   We had a tiring, [?ighty] drive home.
